The first is in metre and the rest in
Or, he should proceed being adorned with diamonds, pearls
and ornaments, wearing white garlands, turban, ointment and
garments, having the royal umbrella held over his head, and sitting
on an elephant, just as planet Venus rides a cloud having the Moon
above him.
[Like Kalidasa in the Raghu (XIII. 76)
l" here our author employs a very charming simile taken
from the planetary region.
The metre is
The king whose army consists of soldiers, elephants and horses
in a happy mood, is bright with the rays of glittering weapons, is
free from unnatural dispositions or evil portents, and is dreadful to
the hosts of the enemies, will conquer ere long the whole earth.
[The metre is toto^Tl].

Chapter XLV—The Wagtail

I shall now expound the effects, enunciated by ancient Sages,
of the first sighting of the bird called Wagtail in the season.
